service_line_config.name: python_dev
service_line_config.tech_context: This project is built with Python. Key considerations: - Web Frameworks: FastAPI, Django, Flask - ORM: SQLAlchemy, Django ORM, Tortoise ORM - Async: asyncio, HTTPX, aiohttp for async HTTP, Celery for background tasks - Data validation: Pydantic for schema validation and serialization - Authentication: OAuth 2.0, JWT, python-jose, Authlib - Messaging: Celery + Redis/RabbitMQ, Kafka (confluent-kafka) - Caching: Redis (redis-py), in-memory caching - Testing: pytest, pytest-asyncio, unittest.mock, factory_boy, httpx for API testing - DevOps: pip/poetry for dependencies, Docker, GitHub Actions, pre-commit hooks - Code quality: mypy, ruff, black, isort - Hosting: Uvicorn/Gunicorn, Docker containers, AWS/Azure/GCP

artifact_overrides.module_extraction: Map modules to Python packages or service boundaries. Each module should correspond to a logical package, app (Django), or router group (FastAPI) in the project structure.
artifact_overrides.functional_req: - Reference Python frameworks and libraries where applicable - For API requirements, specify endpoint patterns and Pydantic schemas - For data requirements, reference ORM models, relationships, and migration approach - For async requirements, specify Celery tasks vs async endpoints
artifact_overrides.nonfunctional_req: - API response time and concurrent request handling capacity - Database connection pooling and query optimization - Celery worker concurrency and task queue management - Memory usage for long-running processes and large dataset handling - Logging and observability (structlog, OpenTelemetry) - Type safety coverage (mypy strict mode targets)
artifact_overrides.task: - Project scaffolding: package structure, dependency management, configuration - Data layer: ORM models, relationships, Alembic/Django migrations - API development: routers/views, Pydantic schemas, dependency injection - Service layer: business logic, repository pattern, error handling - Background tasks: Celery tasks, scheduled jobs, retry policies - Integration: HTTP clients, message producers/consumers, external API wrappers - Testing: pytest fixtures, API tests, mocked dependencies, factory classes - DevOps: Dockerfile, CI pipeline, linting and type checking setup
artifact_overrides.test_case: - Test API endpoints with pytest and HTTPX test client - Test database operations with test database or SQLite in-memory - Test Celery tasks in isolation with eager mode - Test async code with pytest-asyncio - Test business logic with mocked repository dependencies - Verify Pydantic schema validation for edge case inputs
artifact_overrides.architecture: - Project structure: package layout, layer separation, import conventions - API architecture: router organization, middleware, error handling, versioning - Data architecture: ORM models, session management, migration strategy - Task architecture: Celery configuration, task routing, result backends - Integration architecture: HTTP client patterns, retry/circuit-breaker policies - Configuration: environment-based settings, secrets management - Deployment architecture: container setup, ASGI server, worker processes
artifact_overrides.risk_entry: - Python GIL limitations for CPU-bound operations - Dependency conflicts in complex requirements (pip resolution) - Async/sync mixing leading to event loop blocking - ORM N+1 query issues degrading performance - Celery task reliability and dead letter queue handling - Type safety gaps in dynamically typed code paths
